description: Fix progress denominator and zero-scope divisions: align progress reporting with the number of rows actually processed (including skipped rows) and guard against divide-by-zero when scope excludes all rows.
steps: In src/stx/translate/runner.py TranslationProgress.percent property, the guard 'if self.total else 0' already exists - verify it handles total=0
In _emit_progress(), guard the 'rate = completed / elapsed' calculation: if elapsed <= 0, set rate to None
In _emit_progress(), guard the ETA calculation: if rate is None or rate <= 0, set eta to None

In src/stx/gui/pages/phase3_translate.py _on_row_translated(), guard the intermittent summary calculation against self._total_rows == 0 (avoid ZeroDivisionError in 'self._current_row * 100 // self._total_rows')
In phase3_translate.py _on_translation_done(), guard 'rate = done.translated_count / elapsed' against elapsed == 0
Ensure the progress total reflects ALL rows in the document (not just in-scope rows) so the progress bar accurately represents position through the entire dataset
acceptance_criteria: When scope excludes all rows (0 eligible), no ZeroDivisionError occurs
Progress bar correctly reaches 100% when all rows are processed
ETA and rate calculations don't crash on edge cases (0 elapsed, 0 total)
